import functools

from django.conf import settings

from django.contrib.contenttypes.fields import GenericRelation
from django.contrib.contenttypes.models import ContentType

    FieldDoesNotExist,

)

from modelcluster.fields import ParentalKey

    ClusterableModel,
)
from treebeard.mp_tree import MP_Node, MP_NodeManager

from wagtail.actions.copy_for_translation import CopyPageForTranslationAction
from wagtail.actions.copy_page import CopyPageAction
from wagtail.actions.create_alias import CreatePageAliasAction
from wagtail.actions.delete_page import DeletePageAction
from wagtail.actions.move_page import MovePageAction
from wagtail.actions.publish_page_revision import PublishPageRevisionAction
from wagtail.actions.unpublish_page import UnpublishPageAction
from wagtail.compat import HTTPMethod
from wagtail.coreutils import (
    WAGTAIL_APPEND_SLASH,
    camelcase_to_underscore,
    get_supported_content_language_variant,
    resolve_model_string,
    safe_md5,
)
from wagtail.fields import StreamField

from wagtail.query import PageQuerySet

    page_published,
    page_slug_changed,
    pre_validate_delete,
)

from wagtail.utils.deprecation import RemovedInWagtail90Warning

from .sites import Site

from .workflows import WorkflowMixin

logger = logging.getLogger("wagtail")

PAGE_TEMPLATE_VAR = "page"
COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME = getattr(
    settings, "WAGTAIL_COMMENTS_RELATION_NAME", "wagtail_admin_comments"
)


@receiver(pre_validate_delete, sender=Locale)
def reassign_root_page_locale_on_delete(sender, instance, **kwargs):
    # if we're deleting the locale used on the root page node, reassign that to a new locale first
    root_page_with_this_locale = Page.objects.filter(depth=1, locale=instance)
    if root_page_with_this_locale.exists():
        # Select the default locale, if one exists and isn't the one being deleted
        try:
            new_locale = Locale.get_default()
            default_locale_is_ok = new_locale != instance
        except (Locale.DoesNotExist, LookupError):
            default_locale_is_ok = False

        if not default_locale_is_ok:
            # fall back on any remaining locale
            new_locale = Locale.all_objects.exclude(pk=instance.pk).first()

        root_page_with_this_locale.update(locale=new_locale)


PAGE_MODEL_CLASSES = []


def get_page_models():
    """
    Returns a list of all non-abstract Page model classes defined in this project.
    """
    return PAGE_MODEL_CLASSES.copy()


def get_page_content_types(include_base_page_type=True):
    """
    Returns a queryset of all ContentType objects corresponding to Page model classes.
    """
    models = get_page_models()
    if not include_base_page_type:
        models.remove(Page)

    content_type_ids = [
        ct.pk for ct in ContentType.objects.get_for_models(*models).values()

258	    """
259	    Abstract superclass for Page. According to Django's inheritance rules, managers set on
260	    abstract models are inherited by subclasses, but managers set on concrete models that are extended
261	    via multi-table inheritance are not. We therefore need to attach PageManager to an abstract
262	    superclass to ensure that it is retained by subclasses of Page.
263	    """
264	
265	    objects = PageManager()
266	
267	    class Meta:
268	        abstract = True
269

271	# Make sure that this list is sorted by the codename (first item in the tuple)
272	# so that we can follow the same order when querying the Permission objects.
273	PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES = [
274	    ("add_page", _("Add"), _("Add/edit pages you own")),
275	    ("bulk_delete_page", _("Bulk delete"), _("Delete pages with children")),
276	    ("change_page", _("Edit"), _("Edit any page")),
277	    ("lock_page", _("Lock"), _("Lock/unlock pages you've locked")),
278	    ("publish_page", _("Publish"), _("Publish any page")),
279	    ("unlock_page", _("Unlock"), _("Unlock any page")),
280	]
281	
282	PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPE_CHOICES = [
283	    (identifier[:-5], long_label) for identifier, _, long_label in PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES
284	]
285	
286	PAGE_PERMISSION_CODENAMES = [identifier for identifier, *_ in PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES]
287	
288	
289	class Page(AbstractPage, index.Indexed, ClusterableModel, metaclass=PageBase):
290	    title = models.CharField(
291	        verbose_name=_("title"),
292	        max_length=255,
293	        help_text=_("The page title as you'd like it to be seen by the public"),
294	    )
295	    title.required_on_save = True
296	    # to reflect title of a current draft in the admin UI
297	    draft_title = models.CharField(max_length=255, editable=False)
298	    slug = models.SlugField(
299	        verbose_name=_("slug"),
300	        allow_unicode=True,
301	        max_length=255,
302	        help_text=_(
303	            "The name of the page as it will appear in URLs e.g http://domain.com/blog/[my-slug]/"
304	        ),
305	    )
306	    content_type = models.ForeignKey(
307	        ContentType,
308	        verbose_name=_("content type"),
309	        related_name="pages",
310	        on_delete=models.SET(get_default_page_content_type),
311	    )
312	    content_type.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True
313	    url_path = models.TextField(verbose_name=_("URL path"), blank=True, editable=False)
314	    owner = models.ForeignKey(
315	        settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,
316	        verbose_name=_("owner"),
317	        null=True,
318	        blank=True,
319	        editable=True,
320	        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
321	        related_name="owned_pages",
322	    )
323	    owner.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True
324	
325	    seo_title = models.CharField(
326	        verbose_name=_("title tag"),
327	        max_length=255,
328	        blank=True,
329	        help_text=_(
330	            "The name of the page displayed on search engine results as the clickable headline."
331	        ),
332	    )
333	
334	    show_in_menus_default = False
335	    show_in_menus = models.BooleanField(
336	        verbose_name=_("show in menus"),
337	        default=False,
338	        help_text=_(
339	            "Whether a link to this page will appear in automatically generated menus"
340	        ),
341	    )
342	    search_description = models.TextField(
343	        verbose_name=_("meta description"),
344	        blank=True,
345	        help_text=_(
346	            "The descriptive text displayed underneath a headline in search engine results."
347	        ),
348	    )
349	
350	    latest_revision_created_at = models.DateTimeField(
351	        verbose_name=_("latest revision created at"), null=True, editable=False
352	    )
353	
354	    _revisions = GenericRelation(
355	        "wagtailcore.Revision",
356	        content_type_field="content_type",
357	        object_id_field="object_id",
358	        related_query_name="page",
359	        for_concrete_model=False,
360	    )
361	
362	    # Override WorkflowMixin's GenericRelation to specify related_query_name
363	    # so we can do WorkflowState.objects.filter(page=...) queries.
364	    # There is no need to override the workflow_states property, as the default
365	    # implementation in WorkflowMixin already ensures that the queryset uses the

class AbstractPage(
    WorkflowMixin,
    PreviewableMixin,
    DraftStateMixin,
    LockableMixin,
    RevisionMixin,
    TranslatableMixin,
    SpecificMixin,
    MP_Node,
):
    """
    Abstract superclass for Page. According to Django's inheritance rules, managers set on
    abstract models are inherited by subclasses, but managers set on concrete models that are extended
    via multi-table inheritance are not. We therefore need to attach PageManager to an abstract
    superclass to ensure that it is retained by subclasses of Page.
    """

    objects = PageManager()

    class Meta:
        abstract = True


# Make sure that this list is sorted by the codename (first item in the tuple)
# so that we can follow the same order when querying the Permission objects.
PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES = [
    ("add_page", _("Add"), _("Add/edit pages you own")),
    ("bulk_delete_page", _("Bulk delete"), _("Delete pages with children")),
    ("change_page", _("Edit"), _("Edit any page")),
    ("lock_page", _("Lock"), _("Lock/unlock pages you've locked")),
    ("publish_page", _("Publish"), _("Publish any page")),
    ("unlock_page", _("Unlock"), _("Unlock any page")),
]

PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPE_CHOICES = [
    (identifier[:-5], long_label) for identifier, _, long_label in PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES
]

PAGE_PERMISSION_CODENAMES = [identifier for identifier, *_ in PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES]


class Page(AbstractPage, index.Indexed, ClusterableModel, metaclass=PageBase):
    title = models.CharField(
        verbose_name=_("title"),
        max_length=255,
        help_text=_("The page title as you'd like it to be seen by the public"),
    )
    title.required_on_save = True
    # to reflect title of a current draft in the admin UI
    draft_title = models.CharField(max_length=255, editable=False)
    slug = models.SlugField(
        verbose_name=_("slug"),
        allow_unicode=True,
        max_length=255,
        help_text=_(
            "The name of the page as it will appear in URLs e.g http://domain.com/blog/[my-slug]/"
        ),
    )
    content_type = models.ForeignKey(
        ContentType,
        verbose_name=_("content type"),
        related_name="pages",
        on_delete=models.SET(get_default_page_content_type),
    )
    content_type.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True
    url_path = models.TextField(verbose_name=_("URL path"), blank=True, editable=False)
    owner = models.ForeignKey(
        settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,
        verbose_name=_("owner"),
        null=True,
        blank=True,
        editable=True,
        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
        related_name="owned_pages",
    )
    owner.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True

    seo_title = models.CharField(
        verbose_name=_("title tag"),
        max_length=255,
        blank=True,
        help_text=_(
            "The name of the page displayed on search engine results as the clickable headline."
        ),
    )

    show_in_menus_default = False
    show_in_menus = models.BooleanField(
        verbose_name=_("show in menus"),
        default=False,
        help_text=_(
            "Whether a link to this page will appear in automatically generated menus"
        ),
    )
    search_description = models.TextField(
        verbose_name=_("meta description"),
        blank=True,
        help_text=_(
            "The descriptive text displayed underneath a headline in search engine results."
        ),
    )

    latest_revision_created_at = models.DateTimeField(
        verbose_name=_("latest revision created at"), null=True, editable=False
    )

    _revisions = GenericRelation(
        "wagtailcore.Revision",
        content_type_field="content_type",
        object_id_field="object_id",
        related_query_name="page",
        for_concrete_model=False,
    )

    # Override WorkflowMixin's GenericRelation to specify related_query_name
    # so we can do WorkflowState.objects.filter(page=...) queries.
    # There is no need to override the workflow_states property, as the default
    # implementation in WorkflowMixin already ensures that the queryset uses the
    # base Page content type.
    _workflow_states = GenericRelation(
        "wagtailcore.WorkflowState",
        content_type_field="base_content_type",
        object_id_field="object_id",
        related_query_name="page",
        for_concrete_model=False,
    )

    # When using a specific queryset, accessing the _workflow_states GenericRelation
    # will yield no results. This is because the _workflow_states GenericRelation
    # uses the base_content_type as the content_type_field, which is not the same
    # as the content type of the specific queryset. To work around this, we define
    # a second GenericRelation that uses the specific content_type to be used
    # when working with specific querysets.
    _specific_workflow_states = GenericRelation(
        "wagtailcore.WorkflowState",
        content_type_field="content_type",
        object_id_field="object_id",
        related_query_name="page",
        for_concrete_model=False,
    )

    # If non-null, this page is an alias of the linked page
    # This means the page is kept in sync with the live version
    # of the linked pages and is not editable by users.
    alias_of = models.ForeignKey(
        "self",
        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
        null=True,
        blank=True,
        editable=False,
        related_name="aliases",
    )
    alias_of.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True

    search_fields = [
        index.SearchField("title", boost=2),
        index.AutocompleteField("title"),
        index.FilterField("title"),
        index.FilterField("id"),
        index.FilterField("live"),
        index.FilterField("owner"),
        index.FilterField("content_type"),
        index.FilterField("path"),
        index.FilterField("depth"),
        index.FilterField("locked"),
        index.FilterField("show_in_menus"),
        index.FilterField("first_published_at"),
        index.FilterField("last_published_at"),
        index.FilterField("latest_revision_created_at"),
        index.FilterField("locale"),
        index.FilterField("translation_key"),
    ]

    # Do not allow plain Page instances to be created through the Wagtail admin
    is_creatable = False

    # Define the maximum number of instances this page type can have. Default to unlimited.
    max_count = None

    # Define the maximum number of instances this page can have under a specific parent. Default to unlimited.

I now have a complete, verified picture. Here is the audit, worked step by step.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project.** This is **Wagtail**, a Django content-management system (Python backend + a React/TypeScript admin client under `client/src`). ~1,655 files, ~18.4k symbols; Django + React frameworks.

**Package layout** (all under `wagtail/`):
- `wagtail/models/` — core domain models, split by concern: `pages.py` (the page model), `sites.py`, `i18n.py`, `revisions.py`, `draft_state.py`, `locking.py`, `workflows.py`, `reference_index.py`, `audit_log.py`.
- `wagtail/admin/` — the editor-facing CMS (5,610 symbols): views, forms, viewsets, reports, UI panels, rich-text converters.
- `wagtail/api/v2/` + `wagtail/admin/api/` — public and admin REST APIs.
- `wagtail/actions/` — encapsulated page operations (move/copy/delete/publish/alias).
- `wagtail/contrib/` — optional subsystems (forms, redirects, sitemaps, frontend_cache, search_promotions, routable_page, settings, simple_translation).
- `wagtail/permission_policies/`, `wagtail/rich_text/`, `wagtail/templatetags/`, `wagtail/management/commands/`, plus `wagtail/views.py` and `wagtail/signal_handlers.py` at the root.

**The page model you are reworking:** `class Page` at **`wagtail/models/pages.py:289`** — the treebeard `MP_Node` tree node that editors create/move/publish and that gets served to visitors. It is the codebase's central hub (148–200 direct callers, ~790 resolvable graph edges).

## Step 2 — The page model contract

`Page(AbstractPage, index.Indexed, ClusterableModel, metaclass=PageBase)` — `wagtail/models/pages.py:289`. `AbstractPage` (`:248`) mixes in `WorkflowMixin, PreviewableMixin, DraftStateMixin, LockableMixin, RevisionMixin, TranslatableMixin, SpecificMixin, MP_Node`.

**Own fields (`pages.py`):** `title:290`, `draft_title:297`, `slug:298`, `content_type:306` (FK to ContentType — drives `.specific`), `url_path:313`, `owner:314`, `seo_title:325`, `show_in_menus:335`, `search_description:342`, `latest_revision_created_at:350`, `alias_of:392` (self-FK for aliases), plus GenericRelations `_revisions:354`, `_workflow_states:367`, `_specific_workflow_states:381`.

**Tree mechanics (from `MP_Node`):** `path`, `depth`, `numchild` — materialized-path tree; `url_path` is derived from ancestors' slugs (`Page.set_url_path:577`, `_update_descendant_url_paths:889`). `objects = PageManager()` (`BasePageManager(MP_NodeManager)` at `:144`).

**Publishing state (`DraftStateMixin`, `wagtail/models/draft_state.py`):** `live:14`, `has_unpublished_changes:15`, `first_published_at:19`, `last_published_at:22`, `live_revision:25`, `go_live_at:35`, `expire_at:38`, `expired:41`.

**Other contract mixins:** `locked` (`locking.py:12`); `locale` + `translation_key` (`i18n.py:178-179`); `latest_revision` (`revisions.py:254`). `search_fields:402` declares which of these fields the search backend indexes.

**What a teammate must know about how other code holds/queries references:** (a) many satellite models store a page reference as a **FK/GenericRelation** in their own schema (composition — see Step 3); (b) the tree fields `path`/`depth`/`url_path` are read directly by routing, sitemaps, and cache invalidation; (c) `content_type` underpins `.specific` everywhere; (d) the **ReferenceIndex** persists cross-model references generically via post_save/post_delete signals; (e) `Site.root_page` is a hard FK whose `url_path` is cached into site-root-paths.

## Step 4 — Grouped by subsystem

**Serving & site routing** (`wagtail`, `wagtail/models`) — visitor request path. `serve` (`wagtail/views.py:16`) routes a URL to a page and calls `.serve()`; `Site.root_page` FK (`wagtail/models/sites.py:108`), `Site.root_url:186`, `Site.get_site_root_paths:218` read `root_page.url_path`/`locale` to map hostnames→pages.

**Core signal wiring** (`wagtail/signal_handlers.py`) — `pre_delete_page_unpublish:31`, `post_delete_page_log_deletion:38` (page-scoped); `post_save/post_delete_site_signal_handler:23/27` clear the site-root-paths cache; `register_signal_handlers:108` connects them.

**Public + admin API** (`wagtail/api/v2`, `wagtail/admin/api`) — `PagesAPIViewSet.get_base_queryset:518` / `get_root_page:512`; `PagesAdminAPIViewSet.get_base_queryset:92` / `get_root_page:86`. Serialize page fields + tree.

**Admin page management** (`wagtail/admin`) — choosers (`chooser.py:235/440/512`), move (`pages/move.py:66`, `bulk_actions/move.py:12/67`), copy (`pages/copy.py:46`), choose-parent (`pages/choose_parent.py:24`), listing search (`pages/listing.py:435`), forms (`forms/pages.py:15/234/262/283` — slug uniqueness + parent choosers), status side panel (`ui/side_panels.py:268`), viewset registry (`viewsets/pages.py:583`), usage lookup (`admin/models.py:30`).

**Admin reports** (`wagtail/admin/views/reports`) — locked-pages (`locked_pages.py:59`), page-types usage (`page_types_usage.py:27`).

**Permissions** (`wagtail/permission_policies`, `wagtail/models`) — `PagePermissionPolicy.instances_with_direct_explore_permission:162`; `GroupPagePermission` (`pages.py:2147`, FK to page), `PagePermissionTester:2188`, `PageViewRestriction:2470` (FK to page).

**Page actions** (`wagtail/actions`) — `MovePageAction._move_page:35` re-loads and rewrites `url_path` (copy/delete/publish/alias actions imported at `pages.py:41-47`).

**Rich text / linking** (`wagtail/rich_text`, `wagtail/admin/rich_text`) — `PageLinkHandler.get_many:16`, `expand_db_attributes` (`editor_html.py:172`), `PageLinkElementHandler.get_attribute_data` (`html_to_contentstate.py:234`); `slugurl` tag (`templatetags/wagtailcore_tags.py:52`).

**Management commands** (`wagtail/management/commands`) — `fixtree.py:36`, `move_pages.py:12` operate on the tree directly.

**Reference index** (`wagtail/models/reference_index.py`) — `ReferenceIndex:119`, `register_model:290`, `get_references_to:600`; kept in sync by `update_reference_index_on_save:66` / `remove_reference_index_on_delete:79` in `signal_handlers.py`.

**Workflow / audit / notifications** (`wagtail/models/pages.py`) — `WorkflowPage:2537`, `PageLogEntry:2629`, `PageSubscription:2843` — each stores a page FK.

**Contrib models holding a page reference** — forms `AbstractFormSubmission:36` (`contrib/forms`), `Redirect.redirect_page` (`contrib/redirects/models.py:12`), `SearchPromotion:100` (`contrib/search_promotions`), `RoutablePage:221` (subclass overriding `route`).

**Frontend cache** (`wagtail/contrib/frontend_cache`) — `page_published_signal_handler:7`, `page_unpublished_signal_handler:11`, `register_signal_handlers:15` purge CDN URLs on publish/unpublish.

**Sitemaps** (`wagtail/contrib/sitemaps`) — `Sitemap.items:29`, `get_wagtail_site:21`, `views.sitemap:13` walk live pages and call `get_sitemap_urls`.

6. **Admin page management (choosers/move/copy/forms, `wagtail/admin/...`)** — every editor create/move/copy/edit. Many transient queries on `path`/`depth`/`slug`/`url_path`; broad but mostly re-derivable.
7. **Satellite FK holders (`GroupPagePermission`, `PageViewRestriction`, `WorkflowPage`, `PageLogEntry`, `PageSubscription`, `Redirect.redirect_page`, `SearchPromotion`, `AbstractFormSubmission`)** — hold a page reference in their own schema; each needs a migration if the referenced identity/keys change. High per-model coupling, contained scope.
8. **Sitemaps / reports / rich-text / management commands** — lower traffic or admin-only; transient reads of tree + publishing fields.

## Step 6 — Non-obvious dependents (a text search would miss)

- **`wagtail/contrib/frontend_cache/signal_handlers.py:7,11`** — reaches pages only through a **registered signal receiver** on `page_published`/`page_unpublished` (wired in `register_signal_handlers:15`, `apps.py`). No page query in the obvious call graph; config-driven.
- **`wagtail/signal_handlers.py:66,79` (reference index) & `:23,27` (site-root cache)** — connected at app-ready via `connect_reference_index_signal_handlers`/`register_signal_handlers`; they touch page state without a direct `Page.objects` call at the connection site.
- **`wagtail/models/sites.py:108/218`** — depends on the page contract via **`root_page.url_path`/`root_page.locale`**, i.e. it reads page *fields through a FK*, so a grep for `Page` around routing logic misses it.
- **`wagtail/contrib/sitemaps/sitemap_generator.py:29`** — iterates pages via `Site.root_page.get_descendants()`; the coupling is to tree state and `get_sitemap_urls`, not a named `Page` import.
- **`wagtail/models/pages.py:1517` `Page.get_indexed_objects`** + `search_fields:402` — reach the search backend by **declaration**, not a call; changing indexed fields silently changes search behaviour.
- **`wagtail/templatetags/wagtailcore_tags.py:52` `slugurl`** and **rich-text `PageLinkHandler` (`rich_text/pages.py:16`, `admin/rich_text/converters/*`)** — resolve pages by slug/id inside template/HTML rendering, far from the model, keyed on `slug`/`url_path`/`.specific`.
- **`wagtail/contrib/redirects/models.py:12` `Redirect.redirect_page`** and **`contrib/search_promotions/models.py:100`** — live in unrelated contrib apps but hold a page FK in their own schema.
